prompt:
Assign GRADE certainty ratings to each evidence item below.
Ratings: HIGH (systematic review of RCTs, low heterogeneity), MODERATE (RCT with some concerns, or consistent observational), LOW (observational studies, high heterogeneity, indirect evidence), VERY_LOW (case reports, expert opinion, serious limitations).

For each item, output ONLY the number and rating, one per line:
1. HIGH
2. MODERATE
...

EVIDENCE:
1. [GOLD] Source: Influence of Lamination Conditions of EVA Encapsulation on P | Statement: EVA samples with gel content below 70% exhibited low chemical and optical stability, weak adhesion strength, and EVA flowing during aging.
2. [SILVER] Source: Influence of Lamination Conditions of EVA Encapsulation on P | Statement: Laminates with gel content over 92% were more prone to yellowing and exhibited less stable adhesion performance.
3. [SILVER] Source: Encapsulating perovskite solar cells to withstand damp heat  | Statement: Stiff Surlyn encapsulant (394 MPa) adhered well to glass and ITO but caused delamination of perovskite solar cells by pulling up on the ITO layer.
4. [SILVER] Source: Encapsulating perovskite solar cells to withstand damp heat  | Statement: Second-generation glass-glass package with low-modulus polyolefin and 1.5 cm butyl rubber edge seal passed the IEC 61646 1000-hour damp heat test at 8
5. [SILVER] Source: Encapsulating perovskite solar cells to withstand damp heat  | Statement: A bond width of 1.25 cm for butyl rubber with added desiccant was modeled to be adequate for 25-year lifetime against moisture ingress.
